reasoning: The Lean sum `∑ i ∈ Finset.range (p-1), 1/(i+1)` correctly expands to 1/1 + 1/2 + ... + 1/(p-1), matching the informal harmonic sum. The hypotheses (Nat.Prime p, Odd p) capture "odd prime". The universal quantifier over (a,b:ℤ) with a/b = H_{p-1} → p ∣ a is logically equivalent to "p divides the reduced numerator N": if a/b = N/D (reduced), then a = kN, b = kD for some integer k, so p ∣ a for all valid (a,b) iff p ∣ N. The edge cases with b=0 are vacuous since the sum is nonzero. This matches the intended meaning of the informal statement, which asks to show p divides the numerator of the harmonic sum written as a fraction.
